content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 5.1.1(i) WARN — fetched privacy policy (https://runtracker.example/privacy) states "We do not collect, process, or store your location data," but App/RunSessionManager.swift:1-16 imports CoreLocation, requests when-in-use authorization, and stores GPS locations in `route`; the description.txt also advertises GPS run mapping.
Pierre: Apple cross-checks the privacy policy against actual data practices, and a direct contradiction like this is a classic 5.1.1 rejection trigger. The app clearly collects and processes location (it's the core feature), yet the policy explicitly denies it. Update the privacy policy to accurately describe location collection, on-device/iCloud storage, and its purpose before submission.
